title: Uragoga exannulata

intro: Uragoga exannulata, also known as Uragoga, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Convolvulaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are thought to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other infections. The bioactive constituents of Uragoga exannulata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Uragoga is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion in traditional herbal medicine, and may also be used in the form of a topical cream or ointment for its anti-inflammatory effects.
